name: USS Abraham Lincoln
area: Arabian Sea / Gulf of Oman — ~124 nm off southern Iran (BBC satellite imagery Apr 11-12) — forward-most CSG for Epic Fury blockade enforcement
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, F-35C Lightning II (VMFA-314), EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, CMV-22B Osprey, MH-60R/S Seahawk
escorts: DDG-121 USS Frank E. Petersen Jr.
mission_summary: Primary strike platform — blockade enforcement and Hormuz mine clearance support
notes: Day 56: Lincoln remains forward-most CSG for Epic Fury blockade enforcement in the northern Arabian Sea — anchor of the 3-CSG posture announced by CENTCOM Apr 24 (first time 3 U.S. carriers operate simultaneously in the Middle East since Operation Iraqi Freedom 2003). CSG-3 with CVW-9 embarked (including VMFA-314 F-35Cs). DESRON 21 with USS Spruance (DDG-111), USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112), and USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121, Air Defense Cmdr). Combined 3-CSG order of battle in CENTCOM: >200 aircraft, 15,000 sailors/Marines, 9+ DDGs. Iran ceasefire holds (Day 17, no kinetic strikes on Iran since 8 PM ET Apr 7); INDEFINITE extension announced by Trump Apr 21. Defense Sec. Hegseth (Apr 24): blockade will continue 'as long as it takes.' CENTCOM/Navy Times Apr 24: 34 commercial vessels redirected since Apr 13 (SOF News cites 33). Apr 24: Iranian FM Araghchi flew to Islamabad; Witkoff/Kushner depart for Pakistan Apr 25. Pentagon (Apr 22 briefing to Congress): Hormuz mine clearance could take ~6 months. Day 55: Lincoln remains forward-most CSG for Epic Fury blockade enforcement in the northern Arabian Sea (USNI News Fleet Tracker Apr 20 remains latest published; no Day 55 update). CSG-3 with CVW-9 embarked (including VMFA-314 F-35Cs). DESRON 21 with USS Spruance (DDG-111), USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112), and USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121 as ADC). APR 23 ROE ESCALATION: Trump ordered US Navy via Truth Social to 'shoot and kill any boat' laying mines in Strait of Hormuz — most significant rules-of-engagement change since Apr 13 blockade; Pentagon boarded another 'sanctioned stateless vessel' carrying Iranian oil in Indian Ocean overnight Apr 22-23; Reuters reports ≥3 Iranian-flagged tankers intercepted in Asian waters (redirected from near India, Malaysia, Sri Lanka). CENTCOM Apr 23: 31 vessels turned back since Apr 13 (up from 28 Apr 21); ZERO have broken through; operation now involves 10,000+ soldiers, 17 warships, 100+ aircraft. Gen. Caine (JCS Chairman) Apr 23 reaffirmed Adm. Paparo (INDOPACOM) will 'actively pursue any Iranian-flagged vessel or any vessel attempting to provide material support to Iran'; noted 'finite levels' of munitions being expended. Apr 22 Hormuz escalation carried over: IRGC fired on 3 commercial vessels, SEIZED MSC Francesca and Epaminondas. Trump EXTENDED Iran ceasefire INDEFINITELY Apr 21 pending Iranian 'unified proposal' while maintaining blockade. Islamabad Round 2 collapsed Apr 22. US Senate defeated Baldwin war-powers resolution 55-46 Apr 23 (5th failed attempt).
source: Manifest Apr 20, USNI Fleet and Marine Tracker (Apr 13), CENTCOM statement on Touska seizure (Apr 19), CNN/CNBC/Axios/Jerusalem Post/Aviationist

name: USS Gerald R. Ford
area: Red Sea — transited Suez; now over 300 days at sea (longest post-Cold War US carrier deployment)
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets (CVW-8), EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye
mission_summary: Extended Epic Fury deployment — transited Suez into Red Sea — post-Vietnam carrier deployment record broken (>300 days)
notes: Day 56: Ford CSG in central Red Sea — over 300 days deployed (longest post-Cold War US carrier deployment, per Stars & Stripes Apr 24). CVW-8 embarked. Per CENTCOM/USNI News, current Ford CSG escorts include USS Mahan (DDG-72) and USS Winston S. Churchill (DDG-81), in addition to USS Bainbridge (DDG-96) and USS Thomas Hudner (DDG-116). Component of the 3-CSG posture announced by CENTCOM Apr 24 (Lincoln + Ford + Bush) — first time three carriers operate simultaneously in the Middle East since OIF 2003. Iran ceasefire holds (Day 17). Lebanon ceasefire EXTENDED 3 weeks (Trump-brokered Apr 23) but mutual violations confirmed: IDF killed 6 Hezbollah fighters in Bint Jbeil + 2 civilians in Touline Apr 24; Hezbollah shot down Israeli drone, attacked IDF APC. Israeli DefMin Katz (Apr 23): Israel 'awaiting a green light' to resume war and 'return Iran to the dark ages.' Day 55: Ford CSG in central Red Sea off Jeddah — transited Suez southbound Apr 16 (USNI News, TWZ; USNI Apr 20 remains latest tracker). CVW-8 embarked (VFA-31, VFA-37, VFA-87, VFA-213; VAQ-142; VAW-124). DESRON 2 with USS Bainbridge (DDG-96), USS Mahan (DDG-72), plus USS Thomas Hudner (DDG-116). Ford deployment past record 300+ days at sea — longest post-Cold War US carrier deployment. Three-carrier posture: Lincoln (northern Arabian Sea, blockade enforcement), Ford (Red Sea), Bush (transiting Horn of Africa). Trump EXTENDED Iran ceasefire INDEFINITELY ~16:22 ET Apr 21 pending Iranian 'unified proposal'; naval blockade maintained. Islamabad Round 2 collapsed Apr 22. APR 23: Trump ordered 'shoot-and-kill' ROE on Hormuz mine-laying boats (Truth Social); Pentagon boarded another 'sanctioned stateless vessel' overnight; CENTCOM blockade tally up to 31 vessels turned back. Lebanon-Israel Round 2 ambassadors' talks convened in Washington Apr 23 (moved from State Dept to White House; US added Amb. Mike Huckabee; Israel's Yechiel Leiter, Lebanon's Nada Hamadeh Moawad; Rubio expected to join part). IDF Commando Brigade killed 2 armed Hezbollah operatives near Aynata/Bint Jbeil Apr 23. Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon killed Al-Akhbar journalist Amal Khalil (PM Salam: 'war crime'). Houthi activity absent through Apr 23 per corpus — Ford's southern Red Sea positioning retains sea-based strike reach without sitting in the highest-threat cone.
source: Manifest Apr 20, USNI Fleet and Marine Tracker (Apr 13), CENTCOM statement on Touska seizure (Apr 19), CNN/CNBC/Axios/Jerusalem Post/Aviationist

name: USS George H.W. Bush
area: Indian Ocean — CENTCOM AOR (arrived Apr 23 via Cape of Good Hope / Mozambique Channel route, avoiding Bab el-Mandeb)
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, MH-60R/S Seahawk
mission_summary: Third carrier reinforcement for Epic Fury — formally entered CENTCOM AOR Apr 23 — completes 3-CSG posture (first since OIF 2003)
notes: Day 56: BUSH CSG NOW IN CENTCOM AOR — formally announced by CENTCOM via X on Apr 23 (after transiting around southern Africa via Cape Agulhas / Mozambique Channel route, avoiding Bab el-Mandeb). With escorts USS Mason (DDG-87), USS Donald Cook (DDG-75), USS Ross (DDG-71) and DESRON 22 staff; fast oiler USNS Arctic (T-AOE-8). Bush's arrival completes the 3-CSG posture announced by CENTCOM Apr 24 — first time three U.S. carriers operate simultaneously in the Middle East since Operation Iraqi Freedom (2003). Combined 3-CSG order of battle: >200 aircraft, 15,000 sailors/Marines. CSG-10 with CVW-7 embarked, commanded by RADM Alexis Walker. Departed Norfolk Mar 31. Per Pentagon, Avenger-class mine-hunters (pair) departed Japan and were last reported leaving Sri Lanka 'a few days ago' transiting toward CENTCOM (SOF News Apr 24); only confirmed mine-sweeper currently in region: USS Canberra (LCS-30, Independence-class). Pentagon told Congress (Apr 22) clearing Hormuz of Iranian mines could take ~6 months even after hostilities end (DIA assessment 1-6 months). Iran ceasefire holds (Day 17); blockade continues; 34 vessels redirected (CENTCOM Apr 24).
source: Manifest Apr 20, USNI Fleet and Marine Tracker (Apr 13), CENTCOM statement on Touska seizure (Apr 19), CNN/CNBC/Axios/Jerusalem Post/Aviationist

name: USS Tripoli
strike_group: Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group / 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: CENTCOM AOR — Arabian Sea — operational with F-35Bs, ~3,500 personnel
aircraft: F-35B Lightning II (VMFA-121), MV-22B Osprey (VMM-265), AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53K King Stallion
mission_summary: Rapid-response force for Strait of Hormuz — littoral warfare, mine countermeasures, maritime security, potential island seizure operations
notes: Day 53: Tripoli ARG in Arabian Sea; 31st MEU Marines from this force conducted the Apr 19 Touska boarding (rappelled from two MH-60S Seahawks off Tripoli for VBSS). 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines/sailors) embarked, ~20 F-35Bs (VMFA-121) — Tripoli operates as 'Lightning Carrier.' Tripoli ARG (LHA-7 + LPD-18 USS New Orleans + LSD-47 USS Rushmore + 31st MEU) remains forward-positioned for continuing blockade enforcement, littoral warfare, mine countermeasures, potential island seizure ops. USS Boxer (LHD-4) ARG + 11th MEU transiting toward CENTCOM (last confirmed near Guam Apr 11, INDOPACOM AOR). Ceasefire expiry pushed to ~20:00 ET Wed Apr 22. Iran demanded Touska's immediate release Apr 21; Iran FM Baghaei called seizure 'maritime piracy' and a 'war crime.' US delegation (Vance, Witkoff, Kushner) en route to Islamabad for Round 2 talks Apr 22; Iranian attendance unconfirmed EOD Apr 21.
source: Manifest Apr 20, USNI Fleet and Marine Tracker (Apr 13), CENTCOM statement on Touska seizure (Apr 19), CNN/CNBC/Axios/Jerusalem Post/Aviationist

name: Bahrain LCS MCM Squadron
area: Bahrain / Strait of Hormuz — conducting mine countermeasures
mission_summary: Mine countermeasure operations in Strait of Hormuz from Bahrain
notes: Day 37: Three LCS conducting MCM from Bahrain. CRITICAL GAP: Iran possesses estimated 5,000-6,000 naval mines. Last four Avenger-class minesweepers in Bahrain decommissioned Sep 2025. Only 3 LCS with MCM mission packages available; Pentagon OT&E confirmed no operational testing of LCS MCM package in FY2025. Experts estimate clearing Hormuz requires 16 MCM vessels; Navy has 7 total (4 Avengers in Japan). CENTCOM destroyed 44+ minelayers.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker, CRS Report, CENTCOM, UK MoD, Bloomberg, Apr 5 reporting

name: USS Florida
area: Gulf of Oman / Arabian Sea (approx.) — exact position classified
notes: Day 23: Ohio-class SSGN. Carries up to 154 Tomahawk cruise missiles. Submarine operations remain classified — position is approximate/notional. Weapons confirmed in use include Tomahawk Block Va Maritime Strike variant with radar-absorbent 'black' coating. CENTCOM confirms 8,000+ US targets struck cumulative.
source: CENTCOM statements, classification prevents specific confirmation

name: USS John Finn
area: Arabian Sea — operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen
mission_summary: Air defense, escort operations, strike support
notes: Day 23: Part of expanded ~12-14 destroyer presence in region. USS McFaul (DDG-74), USS Milius (DDG-69), USS Delbert D. Black (DDG-119), USS Pinckney (DDG-91), USS Mitscher (DDG-57), USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112), plus 4 DDGs in Eastern Med. Total: ~12-14 destroyers in region.
detachment_note: Operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen in Arabian Sea

name: USS Boxer
strike_group: Boxer Amphibious Ready Group / 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: Western Pacific — departed Pearl Harbor Apr 1; transiting western Pacific; arrival in theater estimated late April to early May
aircraft: AV-8B Harrier II or F-35B Lightning II, MV-22B Osprey, AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53E Super Stallion
mission_summary: Originally scheduled Indo-Pacific; Pentagon indicated may relieve Tripoli's Asia tasking or follow to Middle East
notes: Day 54: Boxer ARG with 11th MEU transiting the Pacific westbound near Guam en route to CENTCOM AOR per manifest Apr 22 (USNI). Boxer carries ~2,500 Marines from Camp Pendleton. Pentagon indicated Boxer would either relieve Tripoli's Asia tasking or follow it to the Middle East. Revised arrival in CENTCOM AOR: late April to early May. Trump EXTENDED Iran ceasefire INDEFINITELY ~16:22 ET Apr 21 while maintaining blockade; Islamabad Round 2 collapsed Apr 22 (Vance trip cancelled, Iran declined); APR 22 HORMUZ SEIZURES by IRGC (MSC Francesca, Epaminondas) keep amphibious capacity in demand for potential island seizure or blockade-support operations.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker, Stars and Stripes, Pentagon, Apr 12 reporting

name: HMS Anson
mission_summary: UK submarine supporting coalition operations in Arabian Sea
notes: Day 29: Royal Navy Astute-class SSN. Departed Perth March 6. Tomahawk-armed. Northern Arabian Sea (reported). No official UK confirmation of exact position.

name: 82nd Airborne Division
area: CENTCOM AOR — undisclosed Middle East locations — 1st BCT 'Devil Brigade' (~2,000 paratroopers)
mission_summary: Ground combat force — 1st BCT Immediate Response Force forward-deployed; speculated mission sets include Kharg Island seizure or Strait of Hormuz operations
notes: Day 44: ~2,000 paratroopers from 1st BCT (Immediate Response Force) remain forward-deployed to undisclosed Middle East locations since March 24-25. ~50,000+ US service members participated in Operation Epic Fury. Forces remain deployed — no drawdown. Islamabad talks collapsed Apr 12 after 21 hours; Trump declared US Navy will blockade Hormuz. 82nd Airborne postured for contingencies if ceasefire collapses after Apr 22 expiration. 60-day War Powers deadline Apr 29. Congress returns Apr 14.
source: Reuters, Stars and Stripes, Pentagon, Apr 12 reporting

name: HMS Dragon
notes: Day 29: Type 45 destroyer. Departed Portsmouth March 10 for air defense mission in Eastern Mediterranean.

name: FS Charles de Gaulle
area: Eastern Mediterranean near Crete/Souda Bay — expanded multinational escort including French, Italian, Spanish, Dutch, German vessels
mission_summary: French carrier operations — Eastern Mediterranean near Crete/Souda Bay. France NOT participating offensively in Epic Fury but has shot down Iranian drones 'in legitimate defense'
notes: Day 41: Charles de Gaulle remains in the eastern Mediterranean near Crete/Souda Bay with an expanded multinational escort including French, Italian, Spanish, Dutch, and German vessels. France is NOT participating offensively in Epic Fury but has shot down Iranian drones 'in legitimate defense.' Macron announced 8 additional frigates and 2 Mistral-class amphibious ships deployed to the region on March 9. French leadership has demanded Lebanon's inclusion in the ceasefire — Macron stated Lebanon was 'fully included.'
source: French MoD / Reuters / Macron statement / Apr 9 reporting

name: VMFA-311 Marine F-35Cs
area: RAF Lakenheath, UK — staging en route to CENTCOM
aircraft: F-35C Lightning II (first 5 of 10 arrived Mar 23-24; first-ever land-based F-35C combat deployment)
mission_summary: Marine F-35C squadron staging through UK en route to CENTCOM theater
notes: Day 27: The War Zone confirmed Mar 24 that first 5 of 10 planned F-35Cs from VMFA-311 'Tomcats' arrived RAF Lakenheath Mar 23-24, having departed MCAS Miramar Mar 10. First-ever land-based combat deployment of the F-35C. Wikipedia states all 10 arrived; second batch of 5 UNVERIFIED in primary reporting. Also notable: AC-130J Ghostrider gunship arrived Lakenheath from Iceland Mar 24.
source: The War Zone Mar 24, USNI News, Pentagon
